title: Asian Heritage Month | A Discussion on Leadership and the Future of Law
content:
## Asian Heritage Month | A Discussion on Leadership and the Future of Law
Asian Heritage Month is an opportunity to recognize and celebrate the diversity, resilience, and contributions of people of all Asian heritages, and to reflect on the importance of representation and inclusion across our professions and communities. Join us for an engaging fireside discussion with some of Canada's leading Asian legal executives, featuring:
- Catherine Lau , General Counsel and Corporate Secretary, MEC
- Miranda Lam , KC , President and Chief Legal Officer, Acuitas Therapeutics, and former McCarthy Tétrault partner
- The conversation will be moderated by Geena Lee , Associate, McCarthy Tétrault
This insightful discussion will explore the panelists’ career journeys, lessons learned along the way, and their thoughts on leadership, opportunity, and the evolving role of lawyers in a rapidly changing business and social landscape. We hope you will join us for this timely conversation.
Note : Instructions on how to access the webinar will be sent a few days before the event. For questions about this event, please contact clientevents@mccarthy.ca . This program qualifies for up to 1 hour of credit under the mandatory education regimes in British Columbia and Ontario and specifically counts towards Ethics/ EDI Professionalism requirements. McCarthy Tétrault LLP has been approved as an Accredited Provider of Professionalism Content by the Law Society of Ontario. Barreau du Québec Continuing Education: This program contains up to 1 hour of content. Three hours of Ethics/Professionalism must be completed directly through the Barreau du Québec. For more information regarding the declaration and recognition of educational activities, please contact the Barreau du Québec .
